items.summary: NSW Police will move to the mandatory use of body-worn cameras for sworn officers in the wake of allegations of a culture of impunity within the police force.

items.title: Trump's intervention in Netanyahu's plans to bomb Beirut reminds world who's calling shots in Iran
items.summary: Donald Trump's intervention in Benjamin Netanyahu's plans to strike Beirut goes to the heart of the relationship between the two sides, in that support may be limited, particularly if it threatens what the US president really wants.

items.title: Kieran Foran named Sea Eagles's full-time NRL coach, Chiefs and Dragons sign Connor Watson
items.summary: Manly shows faith in Kieran Foran by appointing the ex-NRL star as its head coach through to the end of the 2029 season.

items.title: Weather warning for abnormally high tides along entire SA coastline

items.title: Farmers say new rules for feral deer hunting in Tasmania are a 'missed opportunity'
items.summary: The Tasmanian government is making changes to the rules for deer hunting in the state, but farmers say it won't be enough to bring numbers down, with the sector's peak body calling for the species to be declared a pest.

items.title: Quicksand causes terrifying ‘Indiana Jones moment’ for second Adelaide beachgoer
items.summary: Days after a young Adelaide beachgoer had to be rescued from quicksand, a second person has spoken out about a similar experience along the same stretch of coast, saying she was momentarily overcome by "complete fear".

items.title: Health minister asked if nurses have been directed to stop wearing fun scrubs
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Royal Hobart Hospital ban on colourful, 'fun' scrubs for nurses set to be overturned
items.summary: Nurses at the Royal Hobart Hospital have been told they should stop wearing fun scrubs or "free dress" and reminded they need to maintain "some professionalism" regarding their uniform.

items.title: Charge dropped against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones ahead of indecent assault trial
items.summary: Prosecutors drop one of the criminal charges against veteran broadcaster Alan Jones less than two months before the start of his trial, reducing the number of complainants in his indecent assault case.

items.title: NSW releases Cultural Fire Strategy to support Aboriginal-led burning

items.title: Woman credits 'divine intervention' after tree hits car during WA's strongest storm in 49 years
items.summary: A mother shares her incredible tale of survival after a tree falls on her car in a freak accident on a South West highway during WA's strongest storm in 49 years.

items.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine leaves at least 22 dead and dozens injured
items.summary: Moscow targeted Kyiv and other cities, killing multiple people, damaging buildings and leaving wounded trapped under rubble, local authorities say.

items.title: French Open boss Amelie Mauresmo stands firm on line judges after Casper Ruud controversy
items.summary: Despite the other grand slams relying on technology for their line calls, French Open director Amelie Mauresmo is still happy with her human line judges despite a controversial miss costing Casper Ruud.

items.title: ABARES report predicts broadacre farm profits will drop by 70 per cent
items.summary: The ABARES report for the June quarter predicts national crop production will fall by more than 20 per cent, with profits down 70 per cent for broadacre farms.

items.title: Steady is a joy, but raising a dog wasn't what I'd imagined
items.summary: I completely neglected to give much thought to whether my personality would slot into a dog's lifestyle. Spoiler — it did not.

items.title: Fair Work Commission annual ruling lifts minimum wage by 6 per cent and award rate by 4.75 per cent
items.summary: Australia's minimum wage will increase by 5.97 per cent, and minimum award workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay boost, in the Fair Work Commission's (FWC) annual wage review.

items.title: Allegedly stolen vehicle crashes north of Katherine, killing teenage boy
items.summary: A teenager has died after the allegedly stolen vehicle he was in rolled on a major Northern Territory highway early today.

items.title: Meg Lanning gets AI slop treatment in Manchester Super Giants promotional video

items.summary: Australian workers at the Japanese gas company Inpex walked off the job this morning, and plan to strike again this evening.

items.title: Domestic violence survivors tell stories through art in Toowoomba

items.title: Police search for man who fired shots into a house in Melbourne's north last year
items.summary: A man was filmed firing three shots at a house in Melbourne's north in what police believe was a targeted incident last year.

items.title: Non-disclosure agreements to be waived for landmark military sexual violence inquiry
items.summary: Gag orders imposed on former and current Defence personnel who experienced sexual violence will not be enforced, allowing them to participate in a landmark military sexual violence inquiry.

items.title: Race to develop Ebola vaccine heats up amid deadly outbreak in DRC
items.summary: Nearly 250 people are suspected to have died, and more than 1,100 are infected with the rare strain of Ebola in the DRC and Uganda, though the true spread of the virus is feared to be much wider.

items.title: Aryna Sabalenka rolls past Naomi Osaka at French Open as dream runs continue
items.summary: A series of upsets across Roland Garros leaves world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the box seat to win the claycourt grand slam after beating Naomi Osaka.

items.summary: Millions of workers will get a 4.75 per cent pay rise after Fair Work raised modern award wages above the rate of inflation. Catch up on the day's financial news and insights from our specialist business reporters on our blog.

items.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft

items.title: Victorian watchdog set up to monitor farmland restoration amid mineral sands boom

items.title: Council uses AI to inform 10-year plan for 'more depth' at 'fifth of the price'

items.title: Vacant properties across Sydney used as 'meanwhile use' transitional housing for women in need
items.summary: A centuries-old mansion in Sydney became Julie's safe haven, following a series of difficult life circumstances. She is one of many women being assisted through meanwhile use housing.

items.title: Ipswich deputy mayor Nicole Jonic engaged in misconduct over confidential documents, tribunal rules
items.summary: What began as an argument over Paul Pisasale's name on a local bridge evolved into allegations, tribunal hearings and political infighting. A deputy mayor has now been found guilty of misconduct.

items.title: Emirates proposes increase in helicopter flights at luxury Wolgan Valley resort
items.summary: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

items.title: Antibiotic florfenicol detected at 'low level' 14km from fish farm site where it was used
items.summary: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

items.title: Even Hanson knows polling isn't everything but major parties have their work cut out

items.title: How batteries muscled out gas and pushed power prices down

items.summary: As the footballer, shirt torn, face bloodied and with a concussion, paced his police cell, a veteran detective's phone started to ring.

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything

items.title: Scripps Spelling Bee quiz: Do you know what these words mean?
items.summary: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

items.title: Iranian state media reports peace talks with US suspended but Trump insists they continue at 'rapid pace'
items.summary: Iranian state media is reporting that the country's government has halted negotiations with the United States over an end to the war in the Middle East, in protest over Israel's conduct in Lebanon.

items.title: The disturbing role of ChatGPT in mass shootings

items.title: Super Netball Round-Up: 'Awestruck' Grenvold bolts into the Diamonds squad
items.summary: Despite signing her first full-time Super Netball contract this year there was no guarantee Jessie Grenvold would get a lot of court time. But through injury, the Mavericks defender took on a starting spot and is now an Australian Diamond.

items.title: Snowy Monaro Regional Council CEO accuses Andrew Thaler of bullying
items.summary: A Snowy Monaro councillor has accused the industrial relations commission of sanitising his statements in a case over bullying of his council's CEO.

items.title: Euphoria season 3 finale recap: Guns, drugs, the bible and Rue's fate
items.summary: Here's your recap of season 3, episode 8 of Euphoria. There are spoilers. You have been warned.

items.summary: Queensland's task of levelling the State of Origin series has become more difficult after confirmation two of its stars have been ruled out of Game II at the MCG.

items.title: International Ebola cases climbing 'fast' but Australia won't impose travel restrictions
items.summary: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

items.summary: High school students have put on a music festival as part of their studies to get them into the industry.

items.title: Australia wins women's and men's rugby sevens final at World Championship leg in Spain
items.summary: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.

items.title: $6 million of cocaine found near burnt-out truck at boat ramp in Whitsundays
items.summary: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.

items.summary: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

items.title: Thousands of baby green sea turtles head to sea after successful egg relocation
items.summary: The project at Raine Island, where 90 per cent of northern green sea turtles nest, has been working for a decade to improve hatching success.

items.summary: The deaths of two children who were left in cars during hot weather were the result of their parents' memory failure, a NSW coroner finds.

items.title: Mass flowering of Gebang palms in Darwin described as 'once-in-a-lifetime' natural event
items.summary: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

items.title: Tourism operators say AI videos spread 'wrong impression' of WA's north
